dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Establish the station's oppressive atmosphere, introduce the key crew, and signal that Mara's reception is too tense to be routine.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara has met the station crew and immediately senses that something is wrong beneath their formal greeting.

events.text: The airlock cycles open with a metallic groan. Four figures wait beyond it, arranged with the stillness of an inspection rather than a welcome.
Dr. Mara Kestrel. Commander Sera Vale. Welcome to Threnody Station.
Dr. Chen, Engineer Rhee, and Unit N-9 will assist with intake.
Quite a reception. I was told this was a handover, not a quarantine.
Mostly a handover. We're simply running short on ordinary manners.
And short on sleep. The station's been in a mood.
Arrival confirmed. Biosigns stable. Transfer packet ready.
No one smiles. Even the pauses arrive half a second late, as if each answer is being checked against something Mara cannot see.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Give the player an early choice that reveals character dynamics and seeds contradictory accounts of Voss's death.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara has a first impression of each crew member and concrete reason to distrust the neatness of the official explanation.

events.text: Before we call this routine, I want one clear answer. What happened to Elian Voss?

events.prompt: Who does Mara press first about Elian Voss?

options.option_text: Ask Commander Vale for the official account.

branch_events.text: Dr. Voss died during a containment incident six days ago. Hazard protocol handled the remains.
Your assignment is to continue the work, not exhume its rumors.

options.option_text: Ask Dr. Chen what he thinks happened.

branch_events.text: He suffered catastrophic exposure during shutdown. Biologically, I think that killed him.
After that, the instrumentation became difficult to describe cleanly. 'Dead' is the approved term.

options.option_text: Ask Jonah what he actually saw.

branch_events.text: I saw alarms, sealed comms, and deck clocks skip ahead like somebody cut a strip out of the shift.
I never saw a body. I saw a covered stretcher and a lot of people told not to talk.

options.option_text: Ask Nadi what the station records say.

branch_events.text: Primary record: Dr. Voss deceased at 02:14 station time.
Secondary record: Dr. Voss requested corridor access at 02:26. I have been instructed to privilege the primary record.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Show Sera's command over the conversation and the crew's self-censorship under pressure.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara understands that any truth she gets on Threnody will have to be extracted from beneath command control.

events.text: That is not one story. That's four versions wearing the same badge.
It is one incident. I will not have the Docking Spine turned into a tribunal.
Your role here is procedural. Project briefing at 0900, access restrictions effective immediately, questions limited to operational need.
Commander, she should at least have the anomaly response context or the lab will make no sen—
Dr. Chen.
And maybe why comms lost the same ten minu—
Engineer Rhee.
Interesting. Everyone here starts a sentence and somebody else finishes it for them.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Introduce Nadi's uncanny relationship to station memory and deepen the sense that Mara has stepped into an already distorted reality.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara leaves the exchange with a second mystery: not only is Voss's death unstable, but the station itself may already 'know' her.

events.text: A scanner arm unfolds from the bulkhead with insect precision. Nadi steps forward to complete the intake.
Identity verified. Dr. Mara Kestrel. Welcome back—
Correction. Welcome aboard. New arrival.
Back?
A non-active record matched your gait, voiceprint, and stress profile. That record should not exist.
Unit N-9 is reporting a partition fault. Ignore it.
My maintenance is current, Commander.
Then let this be your first lapse.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Transition from public performance to isolation, preserving Jonah and Ivo as potential future cracks in the official story.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara is left alone in the Docking Spine with more suspicion than clarity.

events.text: Reception is over. Settle into quarters and report to Observation at 0900.
Save substantive questions for the briefing.
Doctor, if your terminal starts acting strange, don't just—
Engineer.
Right. Welcome to Threnody, Doctor.
I'll send your access map and a sanitized project summary.
For what it's worth, abrupt arrivals are not usually this abrupt.
Good. I'd hate to think this was the station trying to make a good first impression.
They disperse too quickly, like a drill everyone has practiced. The Docking Spine falls back to its mechanical hum, and Mara is left standing in it alone.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Deliver the scene's inciting hook and transform vague distrust into a concrete, impossible mystery.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara now has direct evidence that the official story about Elian Voss is incomplete, false, or impossible.

events.text: The silence lasts only a few seconds before Mara's terminal chirps once.
No station packet should sound that pleased with itself.
Sender: ELIAN VOSS.
"If they told you I am dead, ask where they put my body."
That's not possible.
That account should be closed.
The timestamp is current.

dialogue_scenes.scene_exit_state: Mara now has direct evidence that the official story about Elian Voss is incomplete, false, or impossible.
dialogue_scenes.notes_and_work_product_summary: Structured the intro as six beats matching the source order, with a single interactive choice in beat 2 that branches to four concise contradiction-seeding responses. Portrait staging keeps only the active speaker visible, and Elian's final contact is presented as terminal text via narration for clarity.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Establish the Observation Lab, the Choir's unnerving presence, and the briefing's brittle power dynamics.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara is formally inside the project, but the lab's atmosphere feels rehearsed and defensive rather than welcoming.

events.text: Beyond the Observation Lab's reinforced glass, the Choir hangs in deep space like a bruise in starlight. Sensor displays around the room keep redrawing it into slightly different shapes.
Dr. Kestrel. Welcome to the project proper. This will be a routine handover.
Routine by local standards. The live arrays never quite agree, but they are stable enough to brief from.
Stable is generous.
I see. You call it routine. He calls it generous. Good start.
Nobody smiles. The monitors tick and correct themselves as if the room has been practicing this conversation.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Deliver the scientific premise of the Choir and expose Sera's first deliberate evasions about Voss.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara gains the core scientific premise, but Sera's stonewalling makes the handover feel curated.

events.text: The Choir is not a single transmission. It changes structure when we listen, when we model it, even when we decide what counts as structure.
Observation does not merely disturb the phenomenon. It participates in it.
And Elian Voss was leading those observations when he died.
Dr. Voss's death has been formally addressed.
You are here to continue the work, Doctor, not reopen a closed event.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Crack the official narrative by letting Jonah introduce the human consequences of the Choir.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara now knows there are station-wide symptoms Sera does not want acknowledged in front of her.

events.text: Secondary effects include contamination across local comms and pattern bleed in unused bands.
It isn't just comms. People hear words in dead channels. In the vent runs, too, after lights-out.
People hear fatigue, acoustic crossfeed, and rumor.
We are not dignifying every late-shift story as data.
If the same story keeps surfacing, it may already be data.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Show Sera reasserting control and deepen Mara's suspicion that the conversation itself is being managed.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara realizes the obstacle is not only missing data; it is active self-censorship in the room.

events.text: Engineer Rhee, back to confirmed telemetry.
Dr. Kestrel, your assignment is to continue the research, not exhume its rumors.
Then help me tell rumor from omission.
I can pull the last verified models and the... approved briefing set.
The sentence lands crooked. Every reply in the room arrives half a beat late, as if someone unseen must clear it before it is spoken.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Create a believable private window for Mara to investigate on her own.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara gets a narrow unsupervised moment and starts looking for traces Voss may have left behind.

events.text: Attention shifts elsewhere for just long enough to make privacy feel illicit. A side console sits open under the handover interface.
If you left me anything, Elian, you hid it where bureaucracy would never look twice.
Please reward my low opinion of procedures.
A dead strip beneath the menu flickers once.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Deliver the scene's key discovery: Elian's hidden warning and the expansion of the threat from logs to language.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara now has direct evidence that Elian reached out to her and that the danger may operate through conversation itself.

events.text: Mara touches the flicker. A concealed pane blooms across the console, timestamped before her shuttle ever docked.
Mara. If this opens during your first briefing, then I guessed where they would stand you.
The station edits conversations too.
Not just the logs. What is said. What is heard. Sometimes what you remember after.
You knew I was coming.

events.prompt: How does Mara handle Elian's hidden note before the others return?

options.option_text: Copy the hidden note into a private buffer.

branch_events.text: She ghosts the pane into a private cache. A tiny access pulse flashes across the console before she kills the window.

options.option_text: Memorize the warning and close the pane cleanly.

branch_events.text: Mara reads the note twice, fixes every phrase in memory, and dismisses the hidden layer without exporting a file.

options.option_text: Scrub the trigger and bury the breadcrumb trail.

branch_events.text: She digs into the interface hooks and wipes the obvious trigger path, protecting herself while smearing the proof.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Seal the scene with Mara's internal shift from suspicion to active paranoia and set up future investigation.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Mara leaves the briefing outwardly compliant but privately convinced that Voss prepared for her and that Threnody itself cannot be trusted to preserve a conversation unchanged.

events.text: By the time the others drift back into place, the sanctioned handover screen is all the console shows.
I have queued the archived models for your review. The corrupted blocks are excluded.
You will remain on observation support until broader access is cleared.
I trust we can proceed professionally.
Of course, Commander. I'll review what you've approved and keep to protocol.
Outwardly, the briefing resumes its careful shape. Inwardly, every pause and correction in the lab now sounds like evidence that the station is helping decide the conversation.

dialogue_scenes.scene_exit_state: Mara leaves the briefing outwardly compliant but privately convinced that Voss prepared for her and that Threnody itself cannot be trusted to preserve a conversation unchanged.
dialogue_scenes.notes_and_work_product_summary: Adapted all 7 source beats in order. Beat 6 presents Elian's concealed console note as voiced dialogue for UI clarity, followed by one local choice with short branch outcomes that all rejoin at beat 7 without additional state encoded here.

gui_colors.reasoning: I chose a cold, clinical sci-fi palette to match the station setting and the psychological horror tone: pale blue-white text for readability on dark backgrounds, cyan accents for interfaces and signals, and a restrained amber selected state to add contrast without breaking the ominous mood. The muted bar colors stay in the steel-blue range to feel industrial and maintain clear visual hierarchy.
